description: Port the macOS Clicky companion (menu-bar AI buddy that sees the screen, talks via TTS, and flies the cursor to UI elements) to Windows. Target stack: C# 12 / .NET 8 / WPF, NAudio for audio, Windows.Graphics.Capture for screenshots, low-level keyboard hook for the global push-to-talk shortcut. The Cloudflare Worker in ../worker is shared and not modified. The Mac reference implementation in ../mac is the source of truth for behavior. All Windows code lands under windows/.

userStories.title: Scaffold Clicky.sln and project layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a buildable .NET 8 solution with the project layout described in windows/README.md so future stories have a place to land.
acceptanceCriteria: windows/Clicky.sln exists and references all planned src/* projects (Clicky.App, Clicky.Companion, Clicky.Audio, Clicky.Capture, Clicky.Hotkey, Clicky.Overlay, Clicky.Api, Clicky.Pointing)
Each project targets net8.0-windows and uses <UseWPF>true</UseWPF> where it owns UI
Clicky.App contains an empty WPF App.xaml with ShutdownMode=OnExplicitShutdown
windows/tests/Clicky.Tests project added with xUnit and a placeholder test that asserts true
dotnet build windows/Clicky.sln succeeds with zero warnings
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-001 - Scaffold Clicky.sln and project layout
userStories.notes: Scaffolded on 2026-04-10. Build verification requires .NET 8 SDK (not available in WSL2 dev environment).

userStories.title: Tray-only host with no main window
userStories.description: As a user, I want Clicky to live in the system tray with no taskbar entry, mirroring the Mac menu bar app behavior driven by leanring_buddyApp.swift + MenuBarPanelManager.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.App boots into a hidden host window (ShowInTaskbar=false, WindowStyle=None, Visibility=Hidden)
H.NotifyIcon (or Hardcodet.NotifyIcon.Wpf) shows a tray icon with tooltip 'Clicky'
Right-click tray menu has Open / Quit entries; Quit calls Application.Current.Shutdown
Left-click tray icon raises an event the Companion layer can subscribe to
App registers itself in HKCU\...\Run on first launch (mirrors SMAppService.mainApp.register in leanring_buddyApp.swift)
dotnet build succeeds and manual smoke test shows the tray icon
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-002 - Tray-only host with no main window
userStories.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-10. Uses Hardcodet.NotifyIcon.Wpf for tray icon, System.Drawing.Common for procedural icon. Build verification requires .NET 8 SDK (not available in WSL2 dev environment).

userStories.title: Settings/control panel WPF window
userStories.description: As a user, I want a popup control panel (mirroring CompanionPanelView.swift) so I can see permission status, current voice state, and a push-to-talk shortcut indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the tray icon toggles a borderless popup window anchored near the tray
Window shows: app version, voice state (Idle/Listening/Processing/Responding), permission rows for Microphone and Screen Capture, and the configured push-to-talk chord
Window auto-hides when it loses focus (Deactivated event)
MVVM: bindings driven by a CompanionViewModel placeholder in Clicky.Companion that exposes the same observable surface as Mac's @Published voiceState/permissions
dotnet build clean
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-003 - Settings/control panel WPF window
userStories.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-10. CompanionViewModel in Clicky.Companion exposes VoiceState + permission bools + push-to-talk chord. CompanionPanelWindow.xaml is a borderless popup anchored near the tray, auto-hides on Deactivated, and binds via DataContext. dotnet build succeeds with 0 warnings.

userStories.title: Microphone permission + WASAPI capture
userStories.description: As a user, I need Clicky to record my microphone in the format AssemblyAI expects, mirroring BuddyDictationManager.swift's AVAudioEngine pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Audio exposes a MicrophoneCapture class using NAudio.Wasapi.WasapiCapture on the default capture device
Captured frames are resampled (NAudio MediaFoundationResampler or custom) to 16 kHz mono PCM16 little-endian
An async IAsyncEnumerable<ReadOnlyMemory<byte>> exposes 50 ms PCM frames suitable for the AssemblyAI websocket
Calls Package.Current.RequestAccessAsync (or falls back to a one-shot WasapiCapture probe) and surfaces a HasMicrophonePermission bool on CompanionViewModel
Unit test in Clicky.Tests verifies the resampler converts a known 48 kHz buffer to the right number of 16 kHz samples
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-004 - Microphone permission + WASAPI capture
userStories.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-10. NAudio.Wasapi 2.2.1 added to Clicky.Audio. MicrophoneCapture wraps WasapiCapture on the default device, downmixes + linearly resamples to 16 kHz mono PCM16 LE, and yields 50 ms frames (1600 bytes) via IAsyncEnumerable<ReadOnlyMemory<byte>>. MicrophonePermissions.ProbeAsync does a one-shot WasapiCapture start/stop as the Win10/11 privacy gate check. App.OnStartup probes in the background and updates CompanionViewModel.HasMicrophonePermission. 5 PcmResampler unit tests (incl. the required 48 kHz → 16 kHz sample-count check). dotnet build + dotnet test: 0 warnings, 6/6 passing.

userStories.title: Low-level keyboard hook for push-to-talk
userStories.description: As a user, I need a system-wide push-to-talk shortcut that fires while Clicky is in the background, mirroring GlobalPushToTalkShortcutMonitor.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Hotkey installs a WH_KEYBOARD_LL hook via SetWindowsHookEx and pumps it on the WPF dispatcher thread
Default chord is Ctrl+Alt (the Win equivalent of macOS ctrl+option) and is configurable via an enum mirroring BuddyPushToTalkShortcut.ShortcutOption
Hook publishes Pressed/Released transitions via an IObservable<ShortcutTransition> or System.Threading.Channels.Channel
Hook is non-blocking and listen-only — never swallows the keys (returns CallNextHookEx)
Hook is uninstalled cleanly on app shutdown; no leaked Win32 handles
Manual smoke test: press Ctrl+Alt with another window focused → CompanionViewModel.IsShortcutPressed flips true on press and false on release
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-005 - Low-level keyboard hook for push-to-talk

userStories.title: Multi-monitor screen capture via Windows.Graphics.Capture
userStories.description: As a user, I need Clicky to capture every connected display as JPEG bytes labeled with which screen the cursor is on, mirroring CompanionScreenCaptureUtility.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Capture exposes ScreenCapture.CaptureAllScreensAsJpegAsync() that returns a list of CapturedScreen { ImageBytes, Label, IsCursorScreen, DisplayBounds, ScreenshotPixelWidth, ScreenshotPixelHeight }
Uses Windows.Graphics.Capture.GraphicsCaptureItem.CreateFromMonitor for each HMONITOR enumerated via EnumDisplayMonitors

Cursor screen is sorted first; label format matches Mac: 'screen N of M — cursor is on this screen (primary focus)' / '… — secondary screen' / 'user's screen (cursor is here)' for the single-display case
Images are resized so the longer edge is 1280 px (matches the Mac maxDimension) and JPEG-encoded at quality ~80
Clicky's own overlay/tray windows are excluded from the capture (use GraphicsCaptureSession.IsBorderRequired = false and filter by HWND when WGC's exclusion API is unavailable)
dotnet build clean; manual smoke test dumps JPEGs to %TEMP%\clicky-capture\*.jpg and the cursor-screen ordering is correct
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-006 - Multi-monitor screen capture via Windows.Graphics.Capture

userStories.title: Claude streaming chat client (SSE)
userStories.description: As a user, I want Clicky to stream Claude's response token-by-token through the existing Cloudflare Worker, mirroring ClaudeAPI.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Api exposes ClaudeClient(string proxyUrl, string model = "claude-sonnet-4-6") with a SendAsync(IReadOnlyList<Message> history, IReadOnlyList<CapturedScreen> screens, string userText, CancellationToken ct) method that returns IAsyncEnumerable<string> token deltas
POSTs to {proxyUrl}/chat with the same JSON shape Mac sends (multi-image content blocks, system prompt, conversation history)
Parses Anthropic SSE event stream (event: content_block_delta with text_delta) and yields incremental text
Auto-detects image media_type by sniffing PNG vs JPEG magic bytes (matches detectImageMediaType in ClaudeAPI.swift)
Warms TLS with a HEAD request on construction to avoid the cold-handshake latency on the first real call (mirrors warmUpTLSConnectionIfNeeded)
Unit test feeds a canned SSE response stream and asserts the deltas come out in order
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-007 - Claude streaming chat client (SSE)

userStories.title: AssemblyAI streaming transcription via WebSocket
userStories.description: As a user, I want my speech transcribed in real time while I hold the push-to-talk key, mirroring AssemblyAIStreamingTranscriptionProvider.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Api exposes AssemblyAiStreamingTranscriber with StartSessionAsync(IReadOnlyList<string> keyterms, ...) returning a session object that exposes PartialTranscriptUpdated and FinalTranscriptReady events
Fetches a short-lived token by POSTing to {proxyUrl}/transcribe-token (token is single-use, never cache)
Opens a ClientWebSocket to streaming.assemblyai.com/v3/ws with the token; sends 16 kHz PCM16 frames from MicrophoneCapture; receives JSON messages and dispatches partial vs final
Reuses a single long-lived HttpClient/ClientWebSocket factory across sessions (Mac's note: per-session URLSession corrupts the connection pool)
Surfaces errors via an OnError callback; never throws on the audio writer task
Manual smoke test: hold push-to-talk, speak a sentence, see partials in the panel and a final transcript on release
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-008 - AssemblyAI streaming transcription via WebSocket

userStories.title: ElevenLabs TTS playback via NAudio
userStories.description: As a user, I want Clicky to speak Claude's response back to me, mirroring ElevenLabsTTSClient.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Api exposes ElevenLabsTtsClient(string proxyUrl) with SpeakAsync(string text, CancellationToken ct)
POSTs to {proxyUrl}/tts with body { text, model_id: 'eleven_flash_v2_5', voice_settings: { stability: 0.5, similarity_boost: 0.75 } }
Decodes the MP3 response with NAudio Mp3FileReader and plays it through WaveOutEvent (or WasapiOut)
StopPlayback() immediately halts current audio so a new utterance can interrupt
IsPlaying property reflects current playback state
Manual smoke test: SpeakAsync("hello from clicky") plays clearly through default output device
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-009 - ElevenLabs TTS playback via NAudio

userStories.title: CompanionManager state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single class that orchestrates push-to-talk → capture → transcribe → Claude → TTS, mirroring CompanionManager.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Companion.CompanionManager subscribes to the hotkey hook from US-005
On Pressed: starts MicrophoneCapture and an AssemblyAI session; sets VoiceState=Listening
On Released: stops capture, awaits final transcript, calls ScreenCapture.CaptureAllScreensAsJpegAsync, sends to ClaudeClient, sets VoiceState=Processing
Streams Claude tokens into a buffer; when streaming finishes, hands the full text to ElevenLabsTtsClient.SpeakAsync and sets VoiceState=Responding; returns to Idle when playback ends
Holds a ConversationHistory list of (userTranscript, assistantResponse) pairs sent on every turn
Cancels any in-flight Claude/TTS task when the user presses the hotkey again (mirrors currentResponseTask cancellation in Mac)
Bound to CompanionViewModel so the panel UI from US-003 reflects state in real time
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-010 - CompanionManager state machine

userStories.title: Transparent click-through overlay window per monitor
userStories.description: As a user, I want a fullscreen, click-through, always-on-top transparent overlay on every monitor where the cursor buddy can render, mirroring OverlayWindow.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Overlay exposes OverlayWindowManager that creates one borderless WPF Window per Screen.AllScreens entry
Each window has AllowsTransparency=true, WindowStyle=None, Background=Transparent, Topmost=true, ShowInTaskbar=false, IsHitTestVisible=false
Sets WS_EX_TRANSPARENT | WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW | WS_EX_NOACTIVATE via SetWindowLong so clicks pass through and the overlay never steals focus
Bounds match the screen's WorkingArea in DIPs (per-monitor DPI aware via app.manifest dpiAwareness=PerMonitorV2)
Reacts to display add/remove (SystemEvents.DisplaySettingsChanged) by re-creating overlays
Overlays are excluded from the screenshot pipeline in US-006 by HWND
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-011 - Transparent click-through overlay window per monitor

userStories.title: Animated blue cursor that flies to a target point
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a glowing blue cursor fly from the system cursor to a target point and point at it, mirroring the BlueCursorView animation in OverlayWindow.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Overlay.BlueCursorControl renders a blue glowing triangle cursor on the overlay window
FlyTo(Point screenPoint, Rect displayBounds, string bubbleText) animates the cursor from the current OS cursor position (GetCursorPos) to screenPoint with a spring/ease curve over ~600 ms
Picks the right monitor's overlay window based on which displayBounds contains the point
Renders a speech-bubble label next to the cursor with the provided bubbleText
After the animation, the cursor lingers ~3 s then fades out (mirrors transientHideTask in CompanionManager)
Smoke test: calling FlyTo with a hardcoded coordinate visibly animates the blue cursor
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-012 - Animated blue cursor that flies to a target point

userStories.title: [POINT:x,y:label:screenN] tag parser + dispatch
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse Claude's pointing tags from the streamed response and feed them to BlueCursorControl, mirroring the Mac flow in CompanionManager.swift / ElementLocationDetector.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Pointing.PointTagParser parses [POINT:x,y:label:screenN] and [POINT:x,y:label] tags out of streamed text and yields PointDirective records
Coordinates are interpreted in the pixel space of the screenshot for the named screen and converted to global desktop DIPs using each CapturedScreen's DisplayBounds + ScreenshotPixelWidth/Height
Stripped text (with all tags removed) is what gets passed to ElevenLabsTtsClient.SpeakAsync
When a PointDirective fires, CompanionManager calls OverlayWindowManager.BlueCursor.FlyTo with the converted screen point + label
Unit tests cover: single screen, multi-screen, missing label, malformed tag (ignored), and the coordinate-conversion math
End-to-end manual test: ask 'where is the close button?' on a focused window and the cursor flies to it
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-013 - [POINT:x,y:label:screenN] tag parser + dispatch

userStories.title: Permissions onboarding flow
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want Clicky to walk me through granting Microphone and Screen Capture access on first launch, mirroring the Mac permissions UI.
acceptanceCriteria: On first launch (no HKCU\Software\Clicky\onboarded value), the panel from US-003 opens automatically
Panel shows step cards for Microphone (prompts via WASAPI access) and Screen Capture (prompts via WGC permission picker)
Each step shows current granted/denied status and a 'Grant' button that triggers the OS prompt
On all-granted, sets HKCU\Software\Clicky\onboarded=1 and dismisses the onboarding view
On subsequent launches, if any permission is missing the panel auto-opens (mirrors CompanionAppDelegate.applicationDidFinishLaunching's allPermissionsGranted check)
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-014 - Permissions onboarding flow

userStories.title: Wire CompanionManager into App startup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the full pipeline to come up on app launch so manual smoke testing works end-to-end, mirroring leanring_buddyApp.swift's launch sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.App App.OnStartup constructs CompanionManager, OverlayWindowManager, and the tray host in the right order
CompanionManager.Start() installs the keyboard hook and is wired to the tray + panel
App.OnExit cleanly stops the hook, cancels any pending Claude/TTS task, and disposes capture sessions
Worker base URL is read from appsettings.json (default: https://your-worker-name.your-subdomain.workers.dev) so users can point at their own deployment without recompiling
Manual end-to-end smoke test: launch app → hold Ctrl+Alt → say 'point at the start menu' → cursor flies to the Start button while Claude's voice plays back
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-015 - Wire CompanionManager into App startup

userStories.title: PostHog analytics + WinSparkle auto-update
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the same telemetry and update story as the Mac app, mirroring ClickyAnalytics.swift and the Sparkle integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicky.Companion.ClickyAnalytics wraps the PostHog .NET SDK with Configure() and TrackAppOpened() entry points called from App.OnStartup
Analytics respects a HKCU\Software\Clicky\analyticsOptOut flag
WinSparkle is initialized at startup pointing at ../mac/appcast.xml's Windows-equivalent feed URL (placeholder is fine; comment notes the maintainer must replace it)
Update check runs once per launch in the background; failures are logged but never block startup
dotnet build clean; manual smoke test confirms a PostHog 'app_opened' event reaches the dashboard with a stub key
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-016 - PostHog analytics + WinSparkle auto-update

userStories.title: Installer / publish pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I need a single .exe (or .msi) I can double-click to install Clicky on Windows 10/11.
acceptanceCriteria: windows/scripts/release.ps1 invokes dotnet publish -c Release -r win-x64 --self-contained false /p:PublishSingleFile=true
Output is wrapped in an MSIX or Inno Setup installer that registers the Run-key entry, places a Start Menu shortcut, and uninstalls cleanly
Installer includes the app.manifest with dpiAwareness=PerMonitorV2 and requestedExecutionLevel asInvoker
README in windows/ documents the build + release commands
Smoke test: install on a clean Win11 VM, launch from Start Menu, complete onboarding, run one push-to-talk turn end-to-end
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-017 - Installer / publish pipeline
